scenario: ChromaDB context layer fills with low-quality proposals that bias future SAGE cycles toward failed strategies
failure_mode: Negative stigmergy: failed pheromone trails should evaporate but don't — they persist and mislead future agents
lesson: Biological stigmergy has evaporation built-in. ChromaDB traces don't evaporate. Must implement explicit trace decay: score each context entry by the quality of proposals it influenced; decay low-quality traces.
counterfactual: If context entries had TTL weighted by downstream proposal success rate, low-quality pheromones would expire within 24h
prevention: Add to sage_context.py: on each SAGE cycle, update context entry weights based on whether proposals citing them became elite or failed. Prune entries with weight < 0.3 after 7 days.
